tender.title: Afghan Temporary Accommodation Support Services
tender.description:
To provide and coordinate support for evacuees temporarily located within Afghan Resettlement and Assistance Policy (ARAP) and Afghan Citizens Resettlement Scheme (ACRS) temporary accommodation within Hampshire.

This Service will provide necessary support and assistance to families who have arrived in the UK and are accommodated temporarily in Home Office sourced Afghan accommodation in the Basingstoke and Deane Borough Council and North Hampshire areas to help them to adjust to life in the UK, until a transfer to permanent housing which could be within their existing local authority or another local authority. Support should be provided in a way that empowers individuals into independence, to build their skills and access services independently and deal with everyday issues themselves.

This is a 12 month contract with an opportunity to extend for a further 6 months.

Additional information: Please note the initial term is for 12 months, with an opportunity to extend for 6 months.
Initial term up until 1/01/2024
6 months extension 01/07/2024

tender.title: Housing Related Support - Mental Health Pathway Services
tender.description:
Haringey Council ("the Council") invites tenders from suitably experienced and qualified providers to deliver the provision of Housing Related Support (HRS) - Mental Health Pathway Services for Haringey residence.

The purpose of the HRS Mental Health Pathway Services is to provide high quality supported accommodation and floating support services for people whose primary need is mental health.  The provision will enable service users to retain and gain the necessary skills to live as independently as possible by building on their strengths and aspirations for the future. The Mental Health Pathway Services also provides a trauma informed service which will support recovery from mental ill health.

The service will be procured in seven (7) lots:

•	Lot 1 - Intensive Support Service
•	Lot 2 - Flexible Support Service - Long Stay
•	Lot 3 - Flexible Support Services - Generic
•	Lot 4 - Flexible Support Service for Women only
•	Lot 5 - Flexible Support Service for Black Men only
•	Lot 6 - Housing First Support Services
•	Lot 7 - Floating Support Services

Tenderers may submit tenders for one, some or all Lots, but no single the service providers shall not be awarded contracts for more than five (5) Lots. 

Any bids received above the budget amount stipulated in attached Contract Notice will not be considered.

Please note that the Tenderers applying for these contracts must have minimum of 3 years proven experience of delivering homelessness prevention and/or housing related support services to Adults whose primary need is mental health.

Additional information: closing date for submission of tenders is 13:00 GMT, Monday 10th May 2021.

tender.title: WP3849a - Temporary Accommodation, Training Flat and Intensive Housing Management for Young People - AWARD
tender.description: The Council is seeking the provision of a range of Supported Housing accommodation and support for Young People who are aged 16-25 and assessed as vulnerable by the Children in Need Service and for who the local authority Children's Services has a Statutory Duty. This includes Children Looked After aged 16-17; Care Leavers aged 18-25; and homeless young people aged 16-18. The units of accommodation will also be used by the Customer Engagement (CE) Team who deal with homeless young people who are aged 16-29. We seek to commission a range of accommodation that will provide the right young people with the right place and support to acquire the skills to live independently, and make successful transition to independent living.

tender.title: Servicing and Maintenance of Passenger Lifts in Social Housing 2022-24
tender.description:
Further competition under SEC framework Passenger and Mobility Lifts Framework lot 1 (OJEU 2019/S 201-488928)

Passenger lift maintenance contract which will cover maintenance and repair of all passenger lifts in F&HDC's social housing portfolio.

The contract will cover the maintenance of passenger lifts on a comprehensive basis with this including planned and preventative maintenance, a responsive call out service, emergency maintenance, repairs and component replacements. Additionally, certain elements of Supplementary Testing will be included within a fixed annual sum.

tender.title: FM22167 - Evaluation of Social Housing Decarbonisation Main Fund
tender.description:
**Please note this is an Award Notice and not a call for competition, this Contract has been awarded via the CCS Research and Insights (RM6126) Dynamic Purchasing System.**

The Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy had a requirement to appoint a contractor to commission process, impact and economic evaluations of Wave 1 and Wave 2.1 of the Social Housing Decarbonisation Fund (SHDF) main fund, a government scheme to improve the energy performance of Social Housing through targeted investment and collaborative engagement with social housing landlords.  

It is anticipated that these evaluations will entail surveys, qualitative interviews, focus groups and secondary data analysis, alongside in-depth analysis of causal mechanisms, outcomes and impacts. BEIS envisages a primarily theory-based approach to impact evaluation, complemented with quasi-experimental analysis of cost and energy savings data, to provide a rigorous understanding of the contribution of SHDF to its intended and unintended outcomes.
tender.procurementMethodDetails: Call-off from a dynamic purchasing system

tender.title: Young People's Supported Accommodation Block Contract(s)
tender.description:
Derby City Council  is seeking to commission a number of  Block Contracts to supply high quality supported accommodation for Young People in care aged 16/17 years, and care leavers up to the age of 21 years. 

The procurement process adopted by the Council for this tender is based on the 'Open' tendering procedure as detailed in the Public Contracts Regulations 2015  but with the flexibility provided by the Light Touch Regime (Regulation s74 to 77) as detailed in Public Contract Regulations 2015.

The contract will run alongside a D2N2 (https://d2n2lep.org/) light touch provider framework, commencing in the Autumn of 2022, which will be used to commission more complex supported accommodation placements when the block contract is unable to meet a Young Person's specific requirements or when the Block Contract has reached full capacity. 

Supported accommodation placements will improve a Young Person's skills in the following areas:

•	Sustaining a tenancy
•	Managing a budget
•	Engaging in the local community
•	Understanding how to access further support
•	Gaining independence skills 

Bidders MUST already have the Accommodation requirements (availability, location and volume) as set out in the specification documents in place at the time of submitting their Bid. 

LOT 1 - CHILDREN IN CARE / LOOKED AFTER CHILDREN
This Lot relates to the following cohorts of Young People:

•	16/17 Year Old Children in Care (CiC) / Looked After Children (LAC) 
•	16/17 Year old Section 20 Young People
•	Unaccompanied Asylum Seeking Children (UASC) awaiting a Home Office decision

Essential Components / Criteria

The Provider must provide accommodation and 10 hours per week of direct one-to-one support per Young Person as outlined further in the Specification.

Placement Number - a total of 40 overall placements per year

LOT 2 - YOUNG HOMELESS / CHILDREN IN NEED / CARE LEAVERS

This Lot relates to the following cohorts of Young People:

•	16/17 Year old Young Homeless 
•	16/17 Year old Children in Need (CiN) 
•	18-21 Year old Care Leavers 
•	18-21 Year old Unaccompanied Asylum Seeking Children (UASC) in receipt of a positive Home Office decision (e.g. granted Leave to Remain)

Essential Components / Criteria

The Provider must provide accommodation and 10 hours per week of direct one-to-one support per Young Person as outlined further in the Specification.

At the point of response to tender, the Provider must provide evidence that they are an approved registered provider of social housing with Derby City Council or that they have previously successfully claimed Housing Benefit from Derby City Council within the last year for 16/17 year olds in supported accommodation in Derby.  

Placement Number - a total of 10 overall placements per year.

Documents are available at www.eastmidstenders.org 

Please search for TD1835.

Please note: we will only accept expressions of interest through the e-tendering system.

Estimated value is £1.175million per annum plus VAT.

Additional information: Please note suppliers wishing to express interest and obtain the documentation must do so by 11.30pm 18/10/2022. It is recommended that you express interest and obtain the documentation well before this time to allow sufficient time to prepare and submit a bid. 

The deadline for returns is 10:00am on Wednesday 19/10/2022.

Documents are available at www.eastmidstenders.org Please search for TD1835.

Please note: we will only accept expressions of interest through the e-tendering system.
